trade.summary: One of the US's most significant bilateral trade relationships, driven by Taiwan's semiconductor and electronics exports. Taiwan is the US's seventh-largest trading partner. Taiwan exports semiconductors, electronics, machinery, and ICT products to the US; the US exports semiconductors equipment, aircraft, agricultural products, and chemicals to Taiwan. Taiwan's TSMC chips are embedded in most US advanced technology systems.

diplomatic.summary: Under the Taiwan Relations Act (1979), the US maintains robust unofficial relations with Taiwan through the American Institute in Taiwan (AIT). The US commitment to Taiwan's defence under strategic ambiguity is a cornerstone of regional stability. High-level US-Taiwan contacts have increased significantly since 2020. The US has supplied arms to Taiwan consistently and has signalled growing resolve to defend Taiwan. Taiwan is treated as a de facto ally in US security planning despite the absence of formal treaty.
recent_incidents: Speaker Pelosi's visit to Taiwan (August 2022) triggered PLA military exercises
US approval of multiple major arms sales packages ($8B+ in 2022-2024)
CHIPS Act investment incentives for TSMC to build US fabs (Phoenix, AZ)

military.summary: Quasi-alliance relationship without a formal treaty. The Taiwan Relations Act obligates the US to provide Taiwan with sufficient defensive arms and maintain the capacity to resist any resort to force. The US has approved billions in arms sales including F-16V fighters, Abrams tanks, PAC-3 Patriot missiles, and Harpoon coastal defence systems. US military officers conduct training exchanges with Taiwan's military. US naval vessels routinely transit the Taiwan Strait asserting freedom of navigation.
incidents: PLA military encirclement exercises following Pelosi visit (2022)
Regular PLA air incursions into Taiwan ADIZ

financial.summary: Exceptionally deep financial relationship. TSMC's investment of $40+ billion in Arizona fabs represents one of the largest foreign investments in US history. US semiconductor equipment firms (Applied Materials, Lam Research, ASML-distributed) are critical suppliers to Taiwan. US private equity and institutional investors hold large Taiwan equity positions. Taiwan holds over $280 billion in US Treasury bonds — among the largest foreign holders.

energy.summary: The US is a major supplier of LNG to Taiwan, helping Taiwan diversify energy imports. Taiwan has committed to increasing US LNG purchases. US energy firms supply Taiwan with LPG, LNG, and crude oil. Cooperation in nuclear energy technology is also present through historical relationships.

scientific.summary: Among the world's most important bilateral technology partnerships. US-Taiwan semiconductor R&D collaboration is uniquely strategic — TSMC, Intel, NVIDIA, and Qualcomm form a deeply integrated ecosystem. Academic collaboration is extensive with tens of thousands of Taiwanese students studying in the US. Joint research in semiconductors, AI, quantum computing, and biotechnology.
